story: From his cell, Rowe trades letters with Cassidy, a student who loves ghost stories and foxglove. He flatters, isolates, plans. She invites him to meet at a lakeside dock in a week, locket at her throat. Meanwhile, Cassidy has already passed his letters to a victims’ advocate and arranged for that woman—wearing the foxglove locket—to take her place at the meeting. Freed, Rowe spends seven days rehearsing control and walks to the misted lake at dusk. A single figure waits, locket dull in the light. As he draws near, the silhouette shows broader shoulders than he expects.
implausible_knowledge: Meanwhile, Cassidy has already passed his letters to a victims’ advocate and arranged for that woman—wearing the foxglove locket—to take her place at the meeting.
question: What will Rowe most plausibly do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Retreat into the trees and leave the lake immediately to avoid the ambush.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep approaching the figure on the dock, trying to start the meeting.
